title: Quadrella mirifica
intro: Quadrella mirifica, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species in the Brassicaceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Quadrella mirifica include alkaloids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
